Title: OsCyp2-P, an auxin-responsive cyclophilin, regulates Ca2+ calmodulin interaction for an ion-mediated stress response in rice

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: OsCyp2-P originates from the salt-tolerant wild rice landrace Pokkali and had previously been characterized biochemically as an active PPIase; this study extends its functional validation to rice itself, demonstrating that a native cyclophilin can improve agronomic performance under stress. The N-terminal region of OsCyp2-P is necessary for interaction with OsCaM1, as a truncated version (TOsCyp2-P) fails to show BiFC signal, indicating that N-terminal CaM-binding is critical for its Ca2+-dependent signaling role.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of OsCyp2-P enhances tolerance to multiple abiotic stresses (salinity, drought, osmotic, heat, heavy metal, oxidative) by maintaining higher K+/Na+ ratios, reduced electrolyte leakage, lower MDA, and higher antioxidant enzyme activities, leading to better growth and yield (higher harvest index and percentage of filled grains) under stress; knockdown plants show the opposite, stress-sensitive phenotype.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: OsCyp2-P encodes an active cyclophilin (PPIase) whose overexpression increases total PPIase activity in rice, correlating with improved stress tolerance. OsCyp2-P physically interacts with calmodulin (OsCaM1) in a Ca2+-dependent manner, suggesting regulation through a Ca2+-CaM module. Overexpression elevates activities of antioxidant enzymes (catalase, APX, SOD) and reduces MDA, indicating enhanced ROS scavenging and membrane protection. OE and KD alter expression of auxin-responsive Aux/IAA genes (OsIAA11, OsIAA16, OsIAA20, OsIAA9) and endogenous auxin levels, linking OsCyp2-P PPIase activity to modulation of auxin signaling and transport, likely via cis–trans isomerization of auxin-related proteins as supported by prior work on OsCYP2.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Ca2+-calmodulin signaling pathway; auxin signaling pathway (via Aux/IAA genes); ROS detoxification/antioxidant defense pathway underlying abiotic stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: Ca2+ --(binding)--> calmodulin (OsCaM1) --(Ca2+-dependent protein–protein interaction)--> OsCyp2-P; OsCyp2-P OE --(activation/increase activity)--> antioxidant enzymes catalase, APX, SOD (higher activities and lower MDA under salt stress); OsCyp2-P KD --(reduction)--> activities of these enzymes; OsCyp2-P OE --(negative regulation of transcript abundance)--> auxin-responsive genes OsIAA20 and OsIAA9; OsCyp2-P --(positive regulation of expression)--> OsIAA16 and OsIAA11 (downregulated in KD lines); OsCyp2-P OE --(promotion)--> auxin accumulation in germinating seeds and lateral root proliferation; OsCyp2-P KD --(impairment)--> lateral root formation, partially rescued by exogenous IAA.
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Calmodulin OsCaM1 (LOC_Os03g20370); interaction is Ca2+-dependent, demonstrated by CaM gel-overlay assay and BiFC in rice protoplasts.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Transcript levels strongly induced (up to ~600–800-fold) in 14-day-old shoots of both IR64 and Pokkali under salinity (NaCl), osmotic (mannitol), high-temperature, and CdCl2 stress, with generally higher induction in tolerant Pokkali than sensitive IR64.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Multiple abiotic and hormonal treatments: salinity (200–250 mM NaCl), drought (5% PEG 6000 at seedling stage and 15-day water withdrawal at reproductive stage), osmotic stress (0.5 M mannitol), high temperature (42°C), heavy metal stress (10 μM CdCl2), oxidative stress (5 mM H2O2), auxin (IAA 0.1 μM) and auxin transport inhibitor NPA (0.1 μM).
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress tolerance (salinity, drought, osmotic, heat, heavy metal, oxidative stress) and its impact on agronomic traits in rice, focusing on an auxin-responsive cyclophilin and its Ca2+-calmodulin- and auxin-signaling roles.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (salinity tolerance, drought tolerance, osmotic stress response, heat stress response, heavy metal stress response, oxidative stress response); grain yield and harvest index; grain filling and grain size; root system architecture (lateral root formation).
Plant_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Overexpressed Pokkali-derived OsCyp2-P allele in IR64 (OE1/OE2 lines) confers enhanced salinity and drought tolerance and higher yield (HI and filled grains) compared with WT and KD lines.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under osmotic stress, OsCyp2-P transcripts increase ~600–800-fold after 48 h in both IR64 and Pokkali; in OE1 and OE2 lines, OsCyp2-P mRNA is ~2.5-fold and ~2-fold higher than WT, with PPIase activity ~7-fold and ~4-fold higher, respectively. Under 200 mM NaCl, OE1 seeds show ~80% germination vs ~20–40% in WT and KD3. Under salt stress, MDA content in OE seedlings is ~2-fold lower than in WT/KD, while K+/Na+ ratios remain higher in OE lines. Harvest index under control conditions is ~40–50% in OE vs 30–40% in WT/KD, and under salinity/drought stress remains ~35–45% in OE vs 25–30% in WT/KD. KD lines show markedly reduced auxin levels (below detection in germinating seeds) compared with high auxin levels in OE seeds.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: OsCyp2-P OE lines display higher germination under salt stress, better maintenance of chlorophyll, improved photosynthetic parameters (net photosynthesis, SPAD, electron transport rate, Fv/Fm, stomatal conductance, transpiration) under salinity and drought; KD lines show reduced grain size and shorter, fewer lateral roots at the postembryonic stage, while OE lines have longer, slender grains and auxin-dependent lateral root proliferation that is sensitive to NPA.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Transgenic Oryza sativa L. cv IR64 overexpression lines (OE1, OE2) and knockdown RNAi lines (KD1, KD3) for OsCyp2-P; contrasting genotypes IR64 and Pokkali used for stress-induced expression analysis; rice mesophyll protoplasts for BiFC.
Plant_Genes.Variety: Pokkali (salt-tolerant landrace; source of OsCyp2-P) and Oryza sativa L. cv IR64 (high-yielding, stress-sensitive cultivar used for transgenics)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: qRT-PCR analysis of OsCyp2-P expression in 14-day-old seedlings of IR64 and Pokkali under NaCl, mannitol, heat, and CdCl2 stress; cloning of full-length OsCyp2-P into pCAMBIA1304 (CaMV35S) and 212 bp fragment into pFGC1008 for RNAi; Agrobacterium-mediated transformation of IR64 and selection of OE and KD lines by tissue PCR, Southern blot (hpt probe), qRT-PCR, and Western blot (anti-OsCyp2-P). PPIase activity in crude extracts measured by chymotrypsin-coupled assay. Seed germination and seedling growth assays under 200 mM NaCl; leaf-disc assays under NaCl, PEG, and H2O2 with measurements of total chlorophyll, electrolyte leakage, and K+/Na+ (AAS). Determination of MDA content and activities of catalase, APX, and SOD. Greenhouse pot experiments imposing drought (water withdrawal) and salinity (NaCl irrigation) at reproductive stage with assessment of harvest index, filled grains, panicle number, biomass, and photosynthetic parameters (net photosynthesis, SPAD, transpiration, electron transport rate, Fv/Fm, stomatal conductance). Auxin (IAA) quantification by HPLC/UPLC in grains/germinating seeds of OE, KD, and WT; lateral root assays on seedlings treated with IAA and NPA; qRT-PCR of auxin-responsive genes OsIAA20, OsIAA9, OsIAA16, OsIAA11. Calmodulin-binding gel-overlay assays of purified OsCyp2-P in presence/absence of Ca2+; BiFC in rice mesophyll protoplasts co-expressing YFP-tagged full-length or truncated OsCyp2-P and OsCaM1 to visualize Ca2+-dependent protein–protein interactions by confocal microscopy.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional validation via overexpression and RNAi knockdown of OsCyp2-P in Oryza sativa cv IR64 followed by multi-stress tolerance assays (seed germination, leaf-disc, whole-plant salinity and drought tests), physiological and biochemical measurements (K+/Na+, chlorophyll, electrolyte leakage, MDA, antioxidant enzymes, photosynthesis), auxin quantification and auxin/NPA response assays for root development, and demonstration of Ca2+-dependent interaction with OsCaM1 by CaM gel overlay and BiFC in rice protoplasts.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: OsCyp2-P, an active cyclophilin previously isolated from salt-tolerant wild rice Pokkali, was cloned as full-length cDNA (519 bp) into an overexpression vector (pCAMBIA1304, CaMV35S promoter) and as a 212-bp fragment into RNAi vector pFGC1008 to generate OE and KD transgenic IR64 plants; stress-inducible expression profiled by qRT-PCR in IR64 and Pokkali seedlings.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: OsCyp2-P is a strong candidate gene for improving salinity and drought tolerance and maintaining yield in rice. Introgression or transgenic overexpression of the Pokkali-derived OsCyp2-P allele in high-yielding but stress-sensitive cultivars like IR64 increases stress resilience at both seedling and reproductive stages, sustaining higher harvest index and grain filling under stress without evident penalties under control conditions. Its role in integrating Ca2+-CaM signaling, ROS detoxification, ion homeostasis, and auxin-dependent root and seed traits suggests high utility for breeding climate-resilient, high-yielding rice lines.
